data.title: Walmart trades in red for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) continued losses for seven straight sessions as the stock closed 1.02% lower, at $94.82 on Thursday. 

The retail giant lost more than 3.5% in the last six trading sessions. WMT is down 1% over the past one month. The stock closed 1.56% lower, at $95.80 on Wednesday. 

Walmart shares have gained more than 4% so far this year, compared to a 2.53% gain in the broader benchmark index. 

Walmart last week announced a major expansion of its drone delivery service. The retail giant said millions of customers in Atlanta, Charlotte, Houston, Orlando, and Tampa will enjoy the convenience of receiving their orders in just minutes.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system has rated Walmart stock as a HOLD, while sell-side analysts see the stock as a STRONG BUY. Meanwhile, SA authors have a SELL rating on the stock. 

Seeking Alpha analyst Kevin George pointed out in a recent analysis that Walmart’s stock has surged since the pandemic, but underlying revenue growth and margins are weakening, raising concerns about sustainability. 

“Recent tariff pressures and management's uncertainty about price elasticity add risk to Walmart's profitability and future performance,” George added.

data.title: MP Materials rallies as Trump eyes Defense Production Act to boost rare earth supply chain
data.description:
MP Materials (NYSE:MP) surged into Thursday's close, finishing +13.9% after Bloomberg reported the Trump administration is developing a plan to use Cold War-era powers to prioritize and fund rare earth projects it deems critical to national security.

Trump officials are said to be discussing using the Defense Production Act to tap financing, loans and other means for rare earths element-related projects, including mining, processing and other downstream technologies to bolster the capability of building a domestic supply chain, although a specific course of action or a timeline are not yet finalized.

MP Materials (NYSE:MP) is poised to be a prime beneficiary, and Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg is working to line up funding for the company, according to the report.

In a congressional hearing earlier this week, Secretary of Defense Hegseth cited MP Materials (NYSE:MP) as "a great example of a place where we can partner with industry" and that Feinberg is focused on sourcing rare earths supply.

data.title: Shopify partners with Stripe for stablecoin payments
data.description:
Payment tech company Stripe (STRIP) announced on Thursday a deeper partnership with the commerce company Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) (TSX:SHOP:CA) to help the latter's merchants accept stablecoin payments.

Stripe said Shopify merchants across 34 countries will be able to accept payments in USDC in a few weeks.

Merchants are expected to have the option to receive stablecoin payments in their preferred local currency or transfer funds as USDC directly to an external wallet.

data.title: Key senator objects to plan to stop Fed interest payments to banks - report
data.description:
Senator Tim Scott (R-SC), the chair of the Senate Banking Committee, said he opposes including a proposal that would prohibit the Federal Reserve from paying interest on reserves to banks in President Donald Trump's tax and spending package, according to a media report.

The proposal, as part of the tax bill called "One Big, Beautiful Bill," would quickly pass through the Senate into law. Rather, Scott said the proposal should follow the typical slow regular committee process, Bloomberg News reported, citing a statement from the Senator.

The ideal is not likely to be incorporated into the tax bill without the committee chair's support.

Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) is pitching the idea to President Trump and Senate Republicans as a means to save $1.1T over a decade, contending that much of the interest goes to foreign banks. Other conservatives backing the concept are Senators Ron Johnson, Rick Scott, and Chip Roy, Bloomberg said.

Missouri Senator Josh Hawley said Wall Street banks' opposition to the proposal makes it more appealing to him. Although he said he doesn't know enough about it to know if it's good policy, he's "very open to it."

Senator Mike Rounds (R-SD) says the concept needs further analysis. He's skeptical of the plan because interest payments are part of the Fed's monetary policy toolkit, the report said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: ARK Invest thinks SpaceX could soar to a $2.5 trillion valuation by 2030
data.description:
ARK Invest created an open-source model that values SpaceX (SPACE) at an expected enterprise value in 2030 of ~$2.5 trillion, which is more than 7X the $350 billion valuation from the last funding round in December 2024.

The Cathie Wood-led firm's analytic model was noted to work like a flywheel with self-reinforcing momentum. Beginning with cash, SpaceX (SPACE) builds rockets and satellites, creates orbital bandwidth, acquires Starlink (STRLK) customers, and reinvests the resulting cash. As the cycle continues, funds flow gradually toward the development of Mars, until the Starlink constellation is complete. 

"When the Starlink constellation is complete, we assume that SpaceX sustains the constellation and then ramps up its investment in Mars. Each Mars-bound rocket carries a combination of Optimus humanoid robots and materials. Assuming Optimus productivity improves and costs decline over time, SpaceX builds its Mars book value."

Once SpaceX (SPACE) completes Starlink’s constellation around 2035, ARK's research suggests that SpaceX (SPACE) could generate ~$300 billion in annual revenue, accounting for ~15% of total spending on global communications.

Elon Musk founded SpaceX with the goal of making life multiplanetary, specifically enabling humans to occupy Mars with the help of his other companies. Per the ARK analyst team, Musk has been engineering Optimus robots and Boring Company machines to build infrastructure for challenging extraterrestrial environments to support the colonization of Mars.

Mutual funds and ETFs that hold SpaceX (SPACE) include ARK Venture Fund (ARKVX), Baillie Gifford U.S. Growth Fund, Scottish Mortgage Investment Trust (OTCPK:STMZF), Edinburgh Worldwide Investment Trust (OTCPK:EWIIF), ERShares Crossover ETF (XOVR), Baron Partners Fund (BPTRX), Destiny Tech100 Fund (DXYZ), and the Fidelity Contrafund (FCNTX).

Accredited investors who meet certain income or net worth thresholds can buy shares directly on private secondary marketplaces such as Hiive, Nasdaq Private Market, ForgeGlobal, and EquityZen. Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Bank of America (BAC) also hold stakes in SpaceX (SPACE), which gives investors in those stocks a very low level of indirect exposure.

data.title: Coinbase unveils its first credit card in partnersip with American Express
data.description:
Crypto exchange Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) will roll out if first branded credit card on the American Express (NYSE:AXP) network, it said on Thursday. The new Coinbase One Card is set to offer up to 4% bitcoin (BTC-USD) back on every purchase.

Rewards will come alongside "unique benefits, offers, experiences, and protections through the American Express Network," Will Stredwick, senior vice president, Global Network Services at American Express said on stage at Coinbase's State of Crypto summit.

The card will be exclusively available to Coinbase One members in the U.S., with the waitlist opened Thursday. Coinbase One members have mounted to over 1M since the monthly subscription product launched in 2023.

"We see real potential in the combination of Coinbase and crypto with the Powerful Backing of American Express, and what the card offers is an excellent mix of what customers are looking for right now," Stredwick said.

data.title: AngloGold rips to multiyear high on rising geopolitical risks; Roth starts coverage at Buy
data.description:
AngloGold Ashanti (NYSE:AU) +6.5% in Thursday's trading to its highest in more than 13 years, as Roth Capital initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $52 price target, citing the miner's "strong plan for growth" through both acquisitions and organic development, as well as the new dividend policy and strong gold price leverage that should make it "an attractive investment for generalist investors."

Roth analyst Joe Reagor highlighted AngloGold's (NYSE:AU) recent efforts to streamline its asset portfolio through expanding Tier 1 assets while divesting smaller operations, pointing to the company's acquisition of Centamin and its Sukari mine in November 2024, followed by the sales of its Côte d’Ivoire projects and Sierra Grande mine.

The development of its Nevada assets "should provide significant production growth" for AngloGold (AU), Reagor wrote in an analysis of the miner's future prospects.

Mining stocks are posting broad gains Thursday on heightened geopolitical risks, with President Trump saying the U.S. was moving personnel out of the Middle East because it "could be a dangerous place."

data.title: Primis Financial plans to sell a portion of Panacea stake for ~$22M
data.description:
Primis Financial (NASDAQ:FRST) announced on Thursday plans to sell a portion of its ownership in the unit, Panacea Financial Holdings, for ~$22M.

The regional lender's deconsolidation of Panacea was effective March 31. FRST had said the deconsolidation enables it to record a pre-tax gain of about $24.6M.

Primis now expects an additional gain of between $6.5M and $7.0M pre-tax as the sales price per share exceeded the carrying value as of March 31.

"Realizing this gain will allow the company to be more aggressive on certain strategies, such as its share repurchase program or accelerating growth," said CEO Dennis Zember.

data.title: Spain's Telefonica is said to eye greater role in defense investments
data.description:
Telefonica (NYSE:TEF) is aiming to expand its footprint in the defense sector as European nations ramp up military spending in response to growing geopolitical tensions, Bloomberg News reported Thursday.

Speaking before Spanish lawmakers, Chairman Marc Murtra emphasized that the telecom giant currently has only a modest presence in defense and security, a level he believes should increase. 

Telefonica already plays a crucial role as a technology and service provider to Spain’s armed forces and the Ministry of Defense. Its strategic relevance has grown following the Spanish state holding company Sepi’s move to become its largest shareholder, a shift that Murtra says positions the company to benefit from rising public sector investment in national defense.

His comments come at a time when Europe is moving to bolster its military capabilities amid ongoing concerns over Russia and uncertainty surrounding U.S. commitment to NATO. Spain alone is expected to boost its defense budget by €10.5 billion ($12.1 billion) this year, reaching NATO's 2% spending target well ahead of schedule.

Sepi took a 10% stake in Telefonica last year, in part to counterbalance Saudi Telecom’s 9.9% ownership. Other major shareholders include Criteria Caixa  and STC.

Murtra, who was appointed chairman in January by the Spanish government, is leading a strategic review of the company. A new roadmap is expected to be unveiled in the second half of 2025. In the meantime, he has repeatedly emphasized the importance of consolidation in the telecom sector and noted that Telefonica is actively exploring acquisition opportunities in Spain, Germany and the United Kingdom.

data.title: Gambling.com Group attracts a Buy rating from Texas Capital
data.description:
Texas Capital Securities initiated coverage on Gambling.com Group Ltd.(NASDAQ:GAMB) with a Buy rating.

The firm sees as Gambling.com (NASDAQ:GAMB) as the go-to affiliate/performance marketing andservices company focused on the online wagering industry. The Jersey-based company's technology and services were noted to be underpinned by visible, multi-year global online wagering total addressable market growth.

"We believe GAMB’s EBITDA will outpace industry TAM growth driven by maturation of the B2C marketing structure, an increase of brands among operators, a longer-term acceleration/mix shift of new iCasino territories, profitable entrance into whitespace jurisdictions, margin augmentation, and continued acquisition integration/execution," highlighted analyst David Bain. 

Bain and his team believe GAMB's valuation should be positively impacted by its recent acquisition of data provider Odds Holdings, which increases the recurring revenue mix and potentially helping solve a key B2C issue of hold volatility.

Looking further ahead, GAMB's business model is seen being profitably transported to significant, untapped international jurisdictions and additional M&A over the next few years is potentially in the mix.

Texas Capital's price target on Gambling.com (NASDAQ:GAMB) of $17 is based on a multiple of 10X the 2025 EV/EBITDA estimate.

Shares of Gambling.com (GAMB) are down 12.8% on a year-to-date basis. The stock has a Strong Buy rating from Seeking Alpha analysts.
